Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Whistler: Live eBPF Programming from the Common Lisp REPL 91 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. CERN to host a new phase of Open Research Europe 219 upvotes, 21 comments
  3. Generators in Lone Lisp 38 upvotes, 2 comments
  4. Show HN: Fio: 3D World editor/game engine – inspired by Radiant and Hammer 67 upvotes, 6 comments
  5. Chroma Context-1: Training a Self-Editing Search Agent 45 upvotes, 3 comments
  6. OpenTelemetry profiles enters public alpha 167 upvotes, 24 comments
  7. Why so many control rooms were seafoam green (2025) 794 upvotes, 149 comments
  8. The European AllSky7 fireball network 38 upvotes, 4 comments
  9. Show HN: Minimalist library to generate SVG views of scientific data 22 upvotes, 2 comments
  10. DOOM Over DNS 276 upvotes, 83 comments
  11. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 891 upvotes, 305 comments
  12. John Bradley, author of xv, has died 262 upvotes, 81 comments
  13. Show HN: I put an AI agent on a $7/month VPS with IRC as its transport layer 235 upvotes, 73 comments
  14. Everything old is new again: memory optimization 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  15. My minute-by-minute response to the LiteLLM malware attack 365 upvotes, 138 comments
  16. HyperAgents: Self-referential self-improving agents 180 upvotes, 65 comments
  17. Agent-to-agent pair programming 68 upvotes, 21 comments
  18. The Legibility of Serif and Sans Serif Typefaces (2022) 39 upvotes, 11 comments
  19. $500 GPU outperforms Claude Sonnet on coding benchmarks 242 upvotes, 106 comments
  20. Using FireWire on a Raspberry Pi 83 upvotes, 38 comments
  21. Chicago artist creates tourism posters for city's neighborhoods 88 upvotes, 41 comments
  22. Anthropic Subprocessor Changes 74 upvotes, 35 comments
  23. A Faster Alternative to Jq 83 upvotes, 42 comments
  24. Suddenly energy independence feels practical:Europeans building mini solar farms 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  25. We haven't seen the worst of what gambling and prediction markets will do 719 upvotes, 499 comments
  26. Colibri – chat platform built on the AT Protocol for communities big and small 119 upvotes, 73 comments
  27. Schedule tasks on the web 134 upvotes, 84 comments
  28. Apple discontinues the Mac Pro 343 upvotes, 270 comments
  29. We rewrote JSONata with AI in a day, saved $500k/year 153 upvotes, 135 comments
  30. 21,864 Yugoslavian .yu Domains 33 upvotes, 37 comments

Last generated 5:30pm, 27 March 2026. Source here.
